meta.title: Level-Crossing & Track-Worker Safe System of Work
meta.subtitle: Named crossing / work site · remedial + protection hierarchies · ALCRM band recorded
meta.author: HSE Rail
meta.deid_notice: All site-safety roles are referred to by role label (COSS, lookout, PICOP, engineering supervisor, crossing keeper). Names and any Sentinel competence-card numbers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document. The ALCRM band shown is RECORDED from the dutyholder's licensed model output — it is never recomputed in this artifact.

sections.text: This safe system of work covers a named level crossing and/or on-or-near-the-line track work. The ALCRM individual/collective risk band is RECORDED from the dutyholder's licensed RSSB/Network Rail model output to prioritise the crossing for remediation; where no band was supplied it is shown as [GAP] and the remedial hierarchy still applies. The band is never invented or recomputed.

metrics.label: ALCRM band (recorded)
metrics.caption: from the licensed model output — never recomputed

metrics.label: Lead crossing control
metrics.caption: closure / grade separation / engineering before signage

sections.text: 2. Level-crossing remedial hierarchy (closure first, signage last)

rows.control: Close the crossing where reasonably practicable (eliminate the conflict)
Grade separation (bridge / underpass) where closure is not reasonably practicable
Engineering protection — barriers / obstacle detection / MSL / full-barrier
Sighting / signage / administrative — LAST, only where a higher order is not reasonably practicable

sections.text: A crossing whose lead control is new signage while closure, grade separation, or engineering is reasonably practicable is rejected — lead with the higher order.
3. Track-worker protection hierarchy (separation first, lookout last)

rows.control: Separation — green-zone working / line blockage / possession (no trains running)
Safe systems of work — planned, authorised system for the task
Warning — TOWS / other warning systems where separation is impractical
Lookout-only (red zone) — LAST resort, where no higher protection is reasonably practicable; minimised

sections.text: A track task whose lead control is a lookout alone while separation (green-zone / line blockage / possession) is reasonably practicable is rejected — keep people and trains apart.

rows.title: [GAP] — populated from the named crossing / work site
rows.description: Each finding is specific to the named site, ranked through the two hierarchies, and traced to evidence.

sections.caption: Residual risk scored after the lead controls are applied

rows.action: [GAP] — each open gap (ALCRM band, NR/L2/OHS/019 issue/date, site specifics) closed by a SMART action with a role owner and a date

items: Network Rail NR/L2/OHS/019 — Safety of People Working On or Near the Line (cited by reference; issue/date [ASSUMED A3] / SME-confirmed)
ORR level-crossing strategic-risk guidance + LXRMTK (Level Crossing Risk Management Toolkit)
ALCRM (All Level Crossing Risk Model) — band RECORDED, never recomputed (RSSB / Network Rail)
ROGS SI 2006/599 + CSM-RA Reg (EU) 402/2013 where a significant change applies
